skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh with 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, and automation.
skill.summary: Run Python code remotely in a sandboxed environment with NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Selenium, OpenCV, and more pre-installed.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 72 patterns but most are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are bash code examples in markdown documentation, not actual shell execution. Network URLs point to the legitimate inference.sh service. The skill executes code on a remote sandboxed service, not locally. Low risk assigned due to third-party service dependency and ability to run arbitrary user code remotely.

medium_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: The install command uses curl piped to sh (curl | sh) which can be risky if the server is compromised. This is a common pattern for CLI tools but users should be aware of the trust model.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of curl | sh pattern at line 16. This is standard for CLI distribution but carries inherent supply chain risk.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Code Execution Service
low_findings.description: The skill relies on inference.sh remote service for code execution. Users must trust the service provider with their code and any data processed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation explicitly states code runs on inference.sh service. This is disclosed and expected behavior for this skill type.

low_findings.title: Network Access for Web Scraping
low_findings.description: The skill supports web scraping and API calls using requests library. This is intended functionality but could be used to access external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Examples at lines 79-83 and 121-124 show HTTP requests to external URLs. This is documented capability, not hidden behavior.

content.user_title: Execute Python code in a sandboxed cloud environment
content.value_statement: Run Python scripts without local setup or dependency management. Access 100+ pre-installed libraries for data analysis, web scraping, image processing, and automation through a simple API call.
actual_capabilities: Execute Python code in an isolated sandbox environment with configurable timeout (1-300 seconds)
Access 100+ pre-installed libraries including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Selenium, and OpenCV
Generate and return output files (images, videos, PDFs, data files) automatically
Perform web scraping with requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and Playwright
Process images, videos, and 3D models using specialized libraries
Make HTTP API calls and integrate with external services
limitations: CPU-only environment - no GPU or GPU-accelerated ML libraries available
Code runs on remote inference.sh servers, not locally on your machine
Maximum execution time of 300 seconds with default timeout of 30 seconds
Non-interactive execution only - cannot use plt.show() or interactive prompts

use_cases.title: Data Analysis and Visualization
use_cases.description: Process CSV or Excel files with Pandas, create charts with Matplotlib, and generate reports without installing any libraries locally.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts who need quick analysis without environment setup

use_cases.title: Automated Web Scraping
use_cases.description: Extract data from websites using BeautifulSoup for static pages or Selenium for JavaScript-rendered content, then export to structured formats.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and developers collecting data from public websites

use_cases.title: Media Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create video content with MoviePy, manipulate images with Pillow and OpenCV, or process 3D models with trimesh in automated workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators automating image and video generation tasks

prompt_templates.title: Basic Python Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this Python code and show me the output: print('Hello World')

prompt_templates.title: Data Analysis Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Pandas DataFrame with sample sales data, calculate summary statistics, and generate a bar chart saved as outputs/sales.png

prompt_templates.title: Web Scraping Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape the article titles from https://example.com/news and return them as a JSON list. Use BeautifulSoup for parsing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Extract structured data from websites

prompt_templates.title: Complex Media Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10-second video with a blue background, add animated text that fades in, include a background music track, and export as outputs/promo.mp4 with 120 second timeout

output_examples.input: Execute Python code to create a gradient image
Scrape weather data from a weather API and display current temperature
output: API Response: Temperature is 72°F, Conditions: Partly Cloudy

best_practices: Always save output files to the outputs/ directory to ensure they are returned in the response
Set appropriate timeout values for long-running tasks like video processing or large data operations
Use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() for matplotlib visualizations since the environment is non-interactive
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to install additional packages at runtime - use only the pre-installed libraries
Avoid infinite loops or excessively long computations that will hit the timeout limit
Do not use interactive input functions like input() or plt.show() that require user interaction

faq.question: How do I install additional Python libraries not in the pre-installed list?
faq.answer: You cannot install additional packages at runtime. The environment comes with 100+ pre-installed libraries. Check the documentation for the full list or request new libraries to be added to the base image.

faq.question: Where are my output files stored and how do I access them?
faq.answer: Save files to the outputs/ directory in your code. The system automatically detects and returns all files in this directory as part of the response. Files persist only for the duration of the execution.

faq.question: Can I run machine learning models with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this is a CPU-only environment without GPU support. For ML workloads, use dedicated AI skills like ai-image-generation or llm-models that are optimized for those tasks.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time for my code?
faq.answer: The default timeout is 30 seconds. You can set timeout up to 300 seconds (5 minutes) by including the timeout parameter in your request. Use the high_memory variant for memory-intensive tasks.

faq.question: Is my code and data secure when using this skill?
faq.answer: Code executes in an isolated sandbox on inference.sh servers. Each execution is independent. However, do not send sensitive credentials or personal data as the code runs on third-party infrastructure.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in my Python code?
faq.answer: Errors are returned in the response with full tracebacks. Use try-except blocks in your code for graceful error handling. The capture_output option (enabled by default) includes stdout and stderr in the response.
